findings.title: MLflow Tracking Server Unauthenticated
findings.description: MLflow tracking server exposed without authentication on port 5000. Full API access to experiments, runs, model registry, and artifact URIs. No credentials required. 81 confirmed instances.

findings.title: CVE-2025-11201 MLflow RCE via Directory Traversal
findings.description: MLflow unauthenticated RCE via directory traversal in model version creation source parameter. Writes arbitrary .pth file to Python sys.path for code execution. No authentication required.

findings.title: CVE-2024-2928 MLflow LFI via URI Fragment
findings.description: MLflow <2.11.3 local file inclusion via artifact URI fragment. GET /model-uri/model-name/artifacts/file#../../../etc/passwd. No authentication required. Reads arbitrary server files.

findings.title: CVE-2024-37052 MLflow RCE via Pickle Model Deserialization
findings.description: MLflow unauthenticated RCE via malicious pickle model upload to model registry followed by model load trigger. Full code execution on MLflow server host.

findings.title: MLflow Arbitrary File Write via file:// artifact_location to /etc/cron.d
findings.description: MLflow artifact_location=file:///etc/cron.d enables arbitrary file write to cron.d via log_artifact API. Live evidence found: experiment pwn_cron_root_d74b03 on 168.138.146.91. Achieves root cron persistence.

findings.title: MLflow Artifact URI S3 Bucket Name Exposure
findings.description: MLflow experiment artifact_location field exposes S3 bucket names in plaintext via unauthenticated API. Example: s3://mlflow-art/ at 103.242.173.183. Enables bucket enumeration and credential pivot.
